sections.paragraph:
ἀθάνατος, ον (Hom.+; ins, pap, LXX; pseudepigr.; Jos., Ant. 11, 56, C. Ap. 2, 277; apolog.) pert. to not being subject to death, immortal of God (Philo, Aet. M. 44; SibOr 3, 276; 582; TestAbr A 17 p. 98, 20 [Stone p. 44]; οἱ ἀ. Hom. et al.) 1 Ti 1:17 v.l. Of the soul (acc. to Diog. L. 1, 24 since Thales and Choerilus Ep. [V b.c.]; s. further Pla., Ep. 7, 335a; Diod S 5, 28, 6; Paus. 4, 32, 4; Herm. Wr. 8, 1; Vett. Val. 242, 16; Iambl., Vi. Pyth. 30, 173; Philo, Op. M. 119.—MNilsson, The Immortality of the Soul in Gk. Rel.: Eranos 39, ’41, 1–16) Dg 6:8. Of γνῶσις (cp. the combin. w. χάρις Demosth., Ep. 4, 9; SIG 798, 7; w. κρίσις OGI 383, 207) 1 Cl 36:2. τὸν ἀ. τῆς ἀναστάσεως καρπὸν τρυγᾶν 2 Cl 19:3. Subst. of Jesus ὁ ἀ. Dg 9:2 (opp. οἱ θνητοί); τὸ ἀ. that which is immortal (opp. τὸ θνητόν, like Philo, Rer. Div. Her. 265) D 4:8 (cp. Alexis 158 τὸ ἀ. in contrast to σῶμα). As name of a horseman Rv 6:8 v.l.—MTreu Griechische Ewigkeitsworter, Glotta 43, ’65, 7.—DELG s.v. θάνατος. TW. Sv.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

pseudepigr. pseudepigr. = pseudepigraphon, pseudepigrapha

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

apolog. apolog. = apologist(s)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

acc. to acc. to = according to

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Thales Thales , s. Vorsokr.; VI b.c.—List 5

Choerilus Choerilus , V b.c.; s. Epici—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Paus Paus , perieg., II a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Iambl Iambl , phil., III–IV a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Gk. Gk. = Greek

Eranos Eranos , Acta Philologica Suecana—List 6

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

combin. combin. = combination

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

2 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 23
